title: Implement junction-tree cache invalidation logic

steps.task: Add CompileResultCache class to inference module
steps.description: Create backend/inference/cache.py with a per-market CompileResult cache that stores compiled artifacts keyed by (market_id, source_state_hash). Supports get, put, invalidate, and stats operations.

steps.task: Integrate cache into server.py compile and query paths
steps.description: Modify compile_market_for_inference() to check cache before recompiling. On cache miss, compile and store. On cache hit, return cached CompileResult. Update query_market_marginals_for_inference() and query_market_atomic_probability_for_inference() to propagate cache_hit status.

steps.task: Wire cache invalidation on ProbabilityEdit and market resolution
steps.description: Modify refresh_market_compile_snapshot() to invalidate the cache for the affected market before rebuilding. This ensures ProbabilityEdit acceptance (201) and market resolution (201) both trigger invalidation.
Increment cache_hits and cache_misses counters in the engine stats when compile results are served from cache vs freshly compiled.

steps.task: Add cache invalidation unit and integration tests
steps.description: Add tests verifying: cache hit on repeated compile, cache invalidation on ProbabilityEdit, cache invalidation on resolution, cache stats tracking, and cache_hit field in query results.

steps.task: Run full test suite and verify all tests pass
steps.description: Run python3 -m unittest discover to verify all existing and new tests pass.

assumptions: Cache is per-market, in-memory, invalidate-on-write (no TTL/LRU)
Cross-market conditional invalidation deferred to T554
EventTrade does not trigger cache invalidation (per T553 freeze)
cache_hit=True returned when serving from cached CompileResult
